AstroBis
Home Solar System Exoplanets ISS Tracker Small Bodies Star Atlas About About Author GitHub
AstroBis research dashboard

A Modern Astronomy Console

AstroBis is built as a browser-native astronomy workspace: part 3D atlas, part data dashboard, and part educational research notebook. It favours traceable public data, careful unit labels, and interactive visualisations that make scale, motion, and uncertainty visible.

Author

Biswajit Jana

Astrophysics and instrumentation researcher focused on exoplanets, spectroscopy, optical systems, scientific computing, and interactive research tools.

Academic portfolio Project report

Oort-scale Solar System atlas with AU ruler, heliopause, and spherical cloud shells

CelesTrak TLE snapshots with local SGP4 propagation and night-side Earth lighting

Exoplanet archive cards plus mission, timeline, and distribution plot windows

JPL close-approach snapshots with interstellar visitor orbit context

Bright-star atlas with RA/Dec, distance shells, spectral color, H-R view, and physical proxies

Data Sources

NASA Exoplanet Archive
Composite exoplanet parameters, stellar metadata, orbit and discovery fields
JPL SBDB CAD
Near-Earth close-approach objects through 2050
JPL SBDB API
Known interstellar and hyperbolic visitor orbit elements
CelesTrak GP/TLE
ISS orbital elements propagated with SGP4
NASA Oort Cloud
Oort Cloud AU scale and outer Solar System context
NASA APOD
Daily astronomy image and scientific context
Solar System Scope / Wikimedia
Planet texture maps where available

Method Notes

The project separates measured data from visual interpretation. Solar-system worlds use public texture maps where possible; exoplanet portraits are explicitly labelled as data-driven artist renders because most exoplanets are not directly resolved in visible light. ISS position is propagated from current TLE data, and small-body risk labels are prioritisation proxies, not official impact predictions.

Biswajit Jana  •  @2026
